label: Peroneal tendinitis without rupture or infection red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated peroneal tendinitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed peroneal tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency pathway today.
home: Rest the sore area as instructed.
Use ice, gentle motion, activity limits, or gradual return to activity only as your clinician recommended.
Avoid activity that causes sharp or worsening pain until you are improving.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not combine medicines unless your clinician said it is safe.
return: Worsening severe pain, new swelling, numbness, weakness, color change, or trouble using the limb.
A pop with loss of strength, new deformity, or concern for tendon rupture.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated peroneal tendinitis.
No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, severe pain, neurovascular compromise, infection concern, inability to use the limb, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, activity modification, symptom care, and warning signs. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed peroneal tendinitis without rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated peroneal tendinitis
no tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, infection, or specialist-directed plan documented
